item.title: History Café: Seattle Mystic Al Hubbard
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Join Brad Holden\, author of Seattle Mystic Alfred M. Hubbard for a discussion of one of Seattle’s most mysterious figures. Hubbard first appeared in the papers in 1919 for his supposed invention of a perpetual motion machine that harnessed energy from Earth’s atmosphere. From there\, he transformed himself into a bootlegger\, radio pioneer\, top-secret spy\, and eventually “the Johnny Appleseed of LSD”. \nHistory Café is produced as a partnership between HistoryLink and MOHAI. \nASL Interpretation and CART captioning are available during the program. In addition\, a limited number of Assistive Listening Devices are available upon request. For more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org two weeks before the program. \nGrab some food for thought–light food and drink will be available to purchase in the atrium. \nIf you can’t make it to the museum\, tune in live on the MOHAI YouTube channel to virtually attend this event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Motorcycle Riding Experience with Cascade Motorcycle Safety
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: South Seattle College North Parking Lot\, 6000 16th Ave SW\, Seattle\, WA\, 98106\, United States

Want to ride a motorcycle but don’t know where to start? Join Cascade Motorcycle Safety and Ride With Us for a chance to experience riding a motorcycle for free\, with professional Cascade Motorcycle Safety RiderCoaches helping you through each step. These 1-on-1\, 20-minute sessions are designed for total beginners\, with bikes and helmets provided. \nThis program is presented with the support of Cascade Motorcycle Safety. \nRide out the remainder of the day in MOHAI to see Kickstands Up! 125 Years of Motorcycling in the PNW. Program participants will receive a coupon for $5 off museum admission. \nAs of 2/18/26\, the location and time of this event has changed.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

Location: Glazer's Camera, South Lake Union, Seattle, WA

Amazing and beautiful landscapes are all around us; they are a joy to experience, but a challenge to capture in a simple photo.

item.title: MOHAI Mercantile Literary Series - Seattle's Locks and Ship Canal
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

MOHAI Mercantile is proud to host the official book launch event for Seattle’s Locks and Ship Canal with local historians David B. Williams and Jennifer Ott! \nThis portable guide from UW Press and HistoryLink is an introduction to the engineering marvel of the Ballard locks\, and a primer on the history of the ship canal. Hear from the authors about the tumultuous history of one of Seattle’s defining projects\, and the dramatic changes it brought to the city’s economy\, neighborhoods\, and natural environment. \nDoors open at 6pm for music and mingling. Food and drink available for purchase. \nConversation and Q&A begin at 7pm\, followed by a book signing with the authors. \nPreorder your copy through the MOHAI Mercantile beginning in January 2026!

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Kickstands Up! Gallery Talk with Pacific Northwest Museum of Motorcycling
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: MOHAI\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Dive deeper into Kickstands Up: 125 Years of Motorcycling in the Pacific Northwest with gallery talks by exhibit co-curators from the Pacific Northwest Museum of Motorcycling. Roll on down to MOHAI and learn more about the bikes and riders that have made history. \nSpots are limited and are assigned on a first-come\, first-served basis. \nFor accessibility support\, please contact programs@mohai.org two weeks before the event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: First Thursday - April
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: MOHAI\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Visit MOHAI on the First Thursday of every month for free admission to the museum’s permanent exhibits between 5—8 pm. \nFree admission includes access to all of MOHAI’s award winning exhibits\, including our temporary displays Riding Together: 135 Years of Cycling in Seattle\, TADAIMA: I’m Home and special exhibit Kickstands Up! 125 Years of Motorcycling in the PNW. Advance online tickets are not required\, but may be reserved below. \nFree admission also includes access to two special presentations of Living Voices in the Joshua Green Theater at 5:30 and 6:30pm. Seats are first-come first-served.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Living Voices: Winning Her Wings
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Joshua Green Foundation Theater\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

They flew planes. They fought prejudice. They made history. Winning Her Wings brings to life the groundbreaking Women Airforce Service Pilots (WASPs). Follow journeys of women who faced and broke barriers together\, fighting for justice in a time of global conflict and domestic prejudice. Discover how they soared—despite the odds. \nThis new multimedia performance by Living Voices blends live theatre and archival film to honor the women who served with courage. Premiering in 2026 for Women’s History Month. This event takes place during our Free First Thursday hours. Seats are first come first served. \nLiving Voices combines live theatrical performances with archival film\, turning history into a moving personal journey. This performance will take place in the Joshua Green Foundation Theater on MOHAI’s second floor.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Living Voices: Winning Her Wings
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Joshua Green Foundation Theater\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

They flew planes. They fought prejudice. They made history. Winning Her Wings brings to life the groundbreaking Women Airforce Service Pilots (WASPs). Follow journeys of women who faced and broke barriers together\, fighting for justice in a time of global conflict and domestic prejudice. Discover how they soared—despite the odds. \nThis new multimedia performance by Living Voices blends live theatre and archival film to honor the women who served with courage. Premiering in 2026 for Women’s History Month. This event takes place during our Free First Thursday hours. Seats are first come first served. \nLiving Voices combines live theatrical performances with archival film\, turning history into a moving personal journey. This performance will take place in the Joshua Green Foundation Theater on MOHAI’s second floor.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: History Café: Preserving the Vision of Asahel Curtis
item.description:
Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Join Margaret Weatherbee\, Head of Collections at the Washington State Historical Society and History Museum\, for an in-depth look at the digitization and preservation of the Asahel Curtis Collection. This collection features over 42\,000 images by photographer Asahel Curtis (1873-1941) that document the evolution of life in the Pacific Northwest in the early 20th century. \nHistory Café is produced as a partnership between HistoryLink\, The Pacific Northwest Historians Guild\, and MOHAI. \nASL Interpretation and CART captioning are available during the program. In addition\, a limited number of Assistive Listening Devices are available upon request. For more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org before the program. \nGrab some food for thought–complimentary snacks and beverages will be provided. \nIf you can’t make it to the museum\, tune in live on the MOHAI YouTube channel to virtually attend this event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: $1 Off Drafts with Seattle Torrent Ticket!
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: TeKu Tavern + Café\, 552 Denny Way\, Seattle\, WA 98109\, USA

Get $1 off All Draft beverages with your Seattle Torrent ticket

From TeKu Tavern

item.title: Meet the Cossacks: Seattle's Motorcycle Stunt and Drill Team
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Microsoft Lakefront Pavilion\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Dive into the history of the Cossacks\, Seattle’s long strolling Motorcycle Stunt and Drill Team\, with Rob Root and other members. Since 1938\, the Seattle Cossacks have captured the imagination of audiences with great pyramids and other wonders of the motorcycling world. Following the presentation\, visitors are welcome to get up close and personal for photos with the team’s vintage Harleys! \nTickets for this program are free\, but do not include access to MOHAI’s exhibitions. If you would like to see all that MOHAI has to offer during your visit\, you can purchase museum admission here. \nFor more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org two weeks before the program.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Paving the Way: Bicycle Infrastructure Tour
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Offsite

Join Merlin Rainwater\, creator of Seattle’s Infamous Redline Guide\, and Tom Fucoloro\, Founder and Editor of the Seattle Bike Blog and author of Biking Uphill in the Rain: The Story of Seattle from behind the Handlebars\, for a bicycle tour exploring the history of Seattle bicycle infrastructure. \nThis tour requires riding a bicycle between multiple sites in Seattle\, totaling close to 10 miles and starting in the Central District. Please bring your own bike. Helmets and lights will be required. For any accessibility support\, or general inquiries\, email programs@mohai.org.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Kickstands Up! All Motorcycle Club Day
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: MOHAI\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Inviting all motorcycle clubs to roll on down to MOHAI! Indicate your Motorcycle Cub membership and receive $5 off admission all day\, and explore Kickstands Up: 125 Years of Motorcycling in the Pacific Northwest with gallery talks by exhibit co-curators from the Pacific Northwest Museum of Motorcycling from 2-4pm. \nSpots are limited and are assigned on a first-come\, first-served basis. \nFor accessibility support\, please contact programs@mohai.org two weeks before the event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Sensory Friendly Morning
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: MOHAI\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

This event welcomes adults and families who may benefit from a low-sensory environment to explore the museum. During this event\, the museum will be closed to the general public and the lights and sounds in the gallery will be lowered. A sensory map and information about what to expect during your visit to MOHAI will be provided via email following registration. Spots are limited so register early! \nHoras más tranquilas\nFecha/hora: sábado 9 de mayo\, de 9:00 a. m. a 11:00 a. m.\nCosto: gratuito con inscripción previa \nEste evento da la bienvenida a adultos y familias que puedan beneficiarse de un entorno con bajo nivel sensorial para explorar el museo. Durante el evento\, el museo permanecerá cerrado al público en general\, y se bajarán las luces y los sonidos de la galería. Tras la inscripción\, se enviará por correo electrónico un mapa sensorial e información sobre lo que puede esperar durante su visita al Museum of History & Industry (MOHAI\, Museo de Historia e Industria). Los cupos son limitados\, así que inscríbase pronto.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Mother's Day Cruises
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Waterways Cruises & Events, South Lake Union, Seattle, WA

Waterways is offering several cruise options throughout the day to help you celebrate Mother’s Day in style!

item.description:
Sunday, May 10, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: The Center for Wooden Boats, South Lake Union, Seattle, WA

A Seattle tradition for more than 25 years!

From Discover South Lake Union Events

Event details

item.title: Behind the Seams: Roaring '20s
item.description:
Saturday, May 16, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Norcliffe Conference Room\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Behind the Seams is back\, with a closer look at the artifacts than ever before! One hundred years later\, women’s fashions of the 1920s continue to shock and to dazzle. Hemlines rose up to the knee and eveningwear were built to move\, shake\, and sparkle. Join Curator of Collections\, Clara Berg for a historical overview and then an intimate viewing of the most stunning 1920s dresses from the museum collection. \nA member pre-sale for this event will begin on March 11 and run until March 23. Secure your spot early by becoming a MOHAI member today! \nFor accessibility support\, contact programs@mohai.org two weeks before the program.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Behind the Seams: Roaring '20s
item.description:
Saturday, May 16, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Norcliffe Conference Room\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Behind the Seams is back\, with a closer look at the artifacts than ever before! One hundred years later\, women’s fashions of the 1920s continue to shock and to dazzle. Hemlines rose up to the knee and eveningwear were built to move\, shake\, and sparkle. Join Curator of Collections\, Clara Berg for a historical overview and then an intimate viewing of the most stunning 1920s dresses from the museum collection. \nA member pre-sale for this event will begin on March 11 and run until March 23. Secure your spot early by becoming a MOHAI member today! \nFor accessibility support\, contact programs@mohai.org two weeks before the program.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Behind the Seams: Roaring '20s
item.description:
Saturday, May 16, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Norcliffe Conference Room\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Behind the Seams is back\, with a closer look at the artifacts than ever before! One hundred years later\, women’s fashions of the 1920s continue to shock and to dazzle. Hemlines rose up to the knee and eveningwear were built to move\, shake\, and sparkle. Join Curator of Collections\, Clara Berg for a historical overview and then an intimate viewing of the most stunning 1920s dresses from the museum collection. \nA member pre-sale for this event will begin on March 11 and run until March 23. Secure your spot early by becoming a MOHAI member today! \nFor accessibility support\, contact programs@mohai.org two weeks before the program.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: History Café: Field Notes Podcast
item.description:
Wednesday, May 20, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Join HistoryLink’s Tony Williams for a live recording of the Field Notes podcast featuring UW Architecture Professor Vikram Prakash. We’re digging into the intersection of deep time and urban design to discover how Seattle’s geological past continues to shape its future. \nHistory Café is produced as a partnership between HistoryLink\, The Pacific Northwest Historians Guild\, and MOHAI. \nASL Interpretation and CART captioning are available during the program. In addition\, a limited number of Assistive Listening Devices are available upon request. For more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org before the program. \nGrab some food for thought–complimentary snacks and beverages will be provided. \nIf you can’t make it to the museum\, tune in live on the MOHAI YouTube channel to virtually attend this event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Drawing From History: Ripples of Time
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Microsoft Lakefront Pavilion\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Lake Union is haunted by ghosts of the past\, echoing through time. In this class\, participants will explore past\, present\, and future of the Lake Union Watershed by creating quick layered gesture drawings in charcoal\, based off photos and ephemera from MOHAI’s collections. The class will culminate at the water’s edge\, creating a drawing representing visions for the future. \nDrawing From History is offered in partnership with Gage Academy of Art. \nAll art making materials will be provided. Tickets include access to MOHAI’s exhibitions. For accessibility support and general program inquiries\, email programs@mohai.org.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: History Café: Shelf Life Community Story Project
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

The Shelf Life Community Story Project records oral histories with current and former residents of Seattle’s Central District neighborhood. Join contributors for a talk about their new book\, Limitless: Stories from the Neighborhood that Shaped Seattle\, the culmination of a multi-year oral history project in Seattle’s historically redlined Central District neighborhood. Stories of innovation\, interdependence\, self-determination\, creativity\, and resistance are illustrated by seven artists with roots in the CD. \nHistory Café is produced as a partnership between HistoryLink\, The Pacific Northwest Historians Guild\, and MOHAI. \nASL Interpretation and CART captioning are available during the program. In addition\, a limited number of Assistive Listening Devices are available upon request. For more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org before the program. \nGrab some food for thought–complimentary snacks and beverages will be provided. \nIf you can’t make it to the museum\, tune in live on the MOHAI YouTube channel to virtually attend this event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details
